cases.answer: The Gold HVAC plan costs $249 per year and includes 2 tune-ups, a 15% repair discount, priority scheduling, and a 1-year labor warranty on repairs, but does not cover T1 repairs. The Platinum plan costs $399 per year and offers unlimited tune-ups, a 20% discount, same-day priority scheduling, a 1-year labor warranty, and covers T1 repairs at no charge. Additionally, Platinum members receive faster emergency response (2 hours or less) compared to Gold (3 hours or less).

cases.answer: Yes, Zelle is accepted as a payment method for residential customers, up to $500.

contexts:
[FAQ — Payments & Invoicing > What payment methods do you accept?]
What payment methods do you accept?
Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Discover · Apple Pay / Google Pay · Personal check (residential) or business check (commercial) · Zelle (residential customers only, up to $500) · Cash (discouraged — technician may not carry change).

[FAQ — Payments & Invoicing > Is financing available?]
Is financing available?
Yes — for jobs over $1,000 via GreenSky. 0% for 12 months with approved credit. Apply online or on-site with the technician.

[FAQ — Payments & Invoicing > When do I pay?]
When do I pay?
Payment is due at completion of service. The technician processes payment on-site via card, tap-to-pay, or check. Net-30 invoicing is available for commercial accounts only.

[FAQ — Payments & Invoicing > Can I get an estimate before the visit?]
Can I get an estimate before the visit?
Phone estimates are ballpark only, based on pricing bands. Written estimates are provided on-site after the diagnostic. Customer must approve before work begins.

judge.reason: The assistant's answer accurately states that Zelle is accepted for residential customers up to $500, which matches the reference. There are no contradictions or fabrications.

cases.answer: Yes, there is an extra charge for booking on Sunday. The after-hours surcharge is $125 on Sundays and federal holidays.

contexts:
[HVAC Pricing Bands > After-Hours Surcharge]
After-Hours Surcharge
+$75 for calls before 7 am or after 6 pm Monday–Saturday.
+$125 on Sundays and federal holidays.

[FAQ — Booking & Scheduling > How far in advance can I book?]
How far in advance can I book?
Up to 60 days. Same-day slots are available before 11 am for Gold and Platinum members, or before 9 am for standard customers.

[Cancellation & Rescheduling Policy > Standard Cancellation Fees]
Standard Cancellation Fees
Notice Given Fee
More than 24 hours before appointment No fee
2 to 24 hours before appointment $35 late-cancel fee
Less than 2 hours or no-show $75 no-show fee (waived once per 12-month period per customer)

[FAQ — Emergencies & After-Hours > After-Hours Surcharges]
After-Hours Surcharges
See the HVAC and Plumbing pricing documents for full surcharge details. Emergency dispatch fees ($99 plumbing,
$89 HVAC) are not waived for after-hours calls.

cases.answer: Yes, you can request a specific technician by using the preferred_tech field when booking. Please note that availability is not guaranteed, and you will be notified if your preferred technician is unavailable.

contexts:
[FAQ — Booking & Scheduling > Can I request a specific technician?]
Can I request a specific technician?
Yes — use the preferred_tech field when booking. Availability is not guaranteed; the system will notify the customer if the preferred tech is unavailable.

[FAQ — Emergencies & After-Hours > How to Request Emergency Service]
How to Request Emergency Service
Call 1-800-555-0190 (24/7). Do NOT use the web booking portal for emergencies — response is not guaranteed
through that channel.

[FAQ — Booking & Scheduling > What is the appointment window?]
What is the appointment window?
All appointments use 2-hour windows (e.g., 10 am – 12 pm). The technician sends an ETA when en route.

[FAQ — Booking & Scheduling > How long does a typical visit take?]
How long does a typical visit take?
Service Type Typical Duration HVAC tune-up 60 – 90 min HVAC diagnostic 45 – 60 min Plumbing service call 60 – 120 min Electrical inspection 60 – 90 min Full system install 4 – 8 hours

cases.answer: Meridian Home Services considers the following issues as emergencies: active water leak or flooding, no heat when the outdoor temperature is forecast below 40°F, no cooling when the indoor temperature exceeds 95°F, electrical hazards such as burning smell, sparking, or partial power loss, and sewage backup.

contexts:
[FAQ — Emergencies & After-Hours > What Counts as an Emergency?]
What Counts as an Emergency?
Active water leak or flooding
No heat when outdoor temperature is forecast below 40°F

Sewage backup
